disclaimer.text: This tool is under active development and may be unreliable. Use at your own risk. Make sure you have a full backup before proceeding.

adb.desc: Communicates with Android devices over USB. Required for detecting your device, sideloading ROMs, and making backups.

heimdall.desc: Flashes firmware to Samsung devices in Download Mode. Required for restoring stock firmware and flashing custom recovery.

wget.desc: Downloads files from the internet. Used to fetch ROM images, firmware, and recovery files.

curl.desc: Transfers data from URLs. Used to check for available ROMs and updates for your device.

lz4.desc: Decompresses LZ4-compressed firmware files. Some Samsung firmware uses this format.

dnsmasq.desc: Network boot server (PXE). Lets you install an OS on another device over the local network without USB.

ipfs.desc: Peer-to-peer file sharing. Optional — lets you store and share ROM files on a decentralized network. Not required for basic use.

3.install: Step 3 of 3 — Install a new OS
3.backup: Step 3 of 3 — Back up your device

category.title: What kind of device?
category.desc: Different devices need different tools. Pick the category that matches your device.

connect.title: Connect your device
connect.desc:
Plug your device into this computer with a USB cable.
Make sure it’s turned on.

help.title: Don’t know how?
help.text:
Use any USB cable that came with your device (or a data cable, not charge-only).
If it’s a phone or tablet, unlock the screen and tap "Allow" if a USB prompt appears.

connect.btn: I plugged it in — detect my device
connect.nodevice: Don’t have a device right now?

goal.title: What would you like to do?

backup.title: Let’s save a backup
backup.desc: We’ll save the important parts of your device so you can restore them later if anything goes wrong.

fix.title: Let’s fix it
fix.desc: We’ll restore the original factory software. This usually fixes boot loops and crashes.

phone.title: Phone or tablet
phone.desc: Samsung, Google Pixel, Xiaomi, Fairphone, PinePhone, and other mobile devices.

computer.title: Computer or SBC
computer.desc: Bootable USB/SD for PCs, Raspberry Pi, Jetson, and single-board computers.

network.title: Router or NAS
network.desc: Flash OpenWrt on routers, PXE boot servers, or network storage devices.

car.title: Car or vehicle
car.desc: Android Auto head units, Raspberry Pi dashboards, and OBD2 devices.

marine.title: Boat or marine
marine.desc: Chart plotters, NMEA 2000 gateways, SignalK hubs, and marine electronics.

iot.title: IoT or wearable
iot.desc: ESP32, smart home devices, IP cameras, PineTime, and other embedded hardware.

console.title: Game console or media
console.desc: Nintendo Switch, Steam Deck, Chromecast, Fire TV, Kindle, and similar devices.

gps.title: GPS or navigation
gps.desc: Garmin, TomTom, drone controllers, and standalone navigation devices.

usb_no_adb.title: Device connected but not visible to ADB
usb_no_adb.hint: Your device is plugged in via USB, but USB debugging is not enabled. Follow these steps on your device:

no_device.hint: No device was detected on USB. Plug in your device and enable USB debugging:

detect.step1: Plug your device into this computer with a USB cable
detect.step4:
Tap Build number 7 times — you'll see "You are now a developer!"
On Android 4.1 or earlier, skip this step — Developer options are already visible in Settings.
detect.step5: Go back to Settings and tap Developer options
detect.step7: If a popup appears on your device asking "Allow USB debugging?", tap Allow
detect.usb_seen: We can see this on USB

multiple.title: Multiple devices detected
multiple.hint: Select the device you want to work with:

found.known: We know this device and have everything ready for it.
found.unknown: Device detected but we don’t have a preset for it yet. You can still continue.

install.title: Install a new operating system
install.desc: Replace the current software with a new one — like LineageOS, /e/OS, or another custom ROM.

backup.title: Back up my device
backup.desc: Save a copy of important system files before making changes. Recommended before installing anything.

fix.title: Fix a broken device
fix.desc: Your device is stuck, boot-looping, or won’t start properly. Let’s bring it back to life.

label.firmware: Factory firmware file (.zip) — leave empty to use the preset if available

placeholder.gapps: Optional: Google Play Store and apps

recovery.detail: Used to repair or reinstall the OS

efs.detail: Extremely important — losing this can disable your SIM card

warning.title: This will erase everything on the device.
warning.desc: All apps, photos, and data on the device will be deleted. If you can still access the device, back up first.

phys1.title: Turn off the device completely
phys1.desc: Hold the power button until the screen goes black.

phys2.title: Enter Download Mode
phys2.desc:
Hold Power + Home + Volume Down at the same time.
When you see a warning screen, press Volume Up to confirm.

phys3.title: Connect the USB cable
phys3.desc: Plug the device into this computer.

stock.title: Stock Firmware
stock.desc: Restore stock Samsung firmware from a ZIP via Heimdall

recovery.title: Custom Recovery
recovery.desc: Flash TWRP or other custom recovery .img via Heimdall

sideload.title: ADB Sideload
sideload.desc: Sideload a custom ROM, GApps, or other ZIP via adb

presets.title: Device Presets
presets.desc: Download firmware, TWRP, ROM, and GApps from devices.cfg presets

detect.title: Auto-Detect
detect.desc: Detect connected device via ADB and match to a preset

backup.title: Backup Partitions
backup.desc: Back up boot, recovery, EFS and other partitions

workflow.title: Full Workflow
workflow.desc: Guided session: stock restore + TWRP + ROM + GApps in one go

magisk.title: Magisk Patch
magisk.desc: Patch boot.img with Magisk for root access

ipfs.title: IPFS Storage
ipfs.desc: Store and retrieve ROMs on IPFS for decentralized sharing

diagnostics.title: Device Diagnostics
diagnostics.desc: Battery health, storage, bootloader status, OS info, and more

community.title: Community Links
community.desc: Forums, wikis, repair guides, and specs for your device

changelog.title: Version Tracker
changelog.desc: Compare installed vs. latest ROM versions with build history

companion.title: Companion Tools
companion.desc: Android scripts, platform tools, and helper apps

stock.title: Restore Stock Firmware
stock.label: Path to firmware ZIP
stock.warn: Ensure the device is in Download Mode before starting (Power + Home + Vol Down).

recovery.title: Flash Custom Recovery
recovery.label: Path to recovery .img
recovery.warn: Ensure the device is in Download Mode before starting.

sideload.title: ADB Sideload
sideload.label: Path to ZIP file
sideload.warn: Start ADB sideload on the device first (TWRP → Advanced → ADB Sideload).

presets.title: Device Presets
presets.desc: Select a device to download firmware, recovery, and ROM files.

detect.title: Auto-Detect Device
detect.desc: Connect your device via USB with USB debugging enabled, or boot into recovery.

backup.title: Backup Partitions
backup.label: Partitions to back up
backup.note: Requires ADB connection. Root access needed for raw partition dumps.

updates.title: Check ROM Updates
updates.desc: Checks SourceForge for the latest LineageOS and /e/OS builds for all configured devices.

workflow.title: Full Workflow
workflow.desc: Guided 4-step session. Leave a field empty to skip that step.

magisk.title: Magisk Patch
magisk.label: Path to boot.img
magisk.flash: Flash patched image via Heimdall after patching
magisk.warn: Requires Magisk app installed on the device and ADB connection.

diagnostics.title: Device Diagnostics
diagnostics.desc: Connect your device via USB with USB debugging enabled to view diagnostics.

companion.title: Companion Tools
companion.desc: Helper apps and scripts for your device and other platforms.

configurator.title: Pre-Flash Configuration
configurator.desc: These settings will be applied automatically after the ROM is installed and the device boots up.

lineage.name: LineageOS
lineage.desc: Popular, open-source Android. Fast and clean.

postmarketos.name: postmarketOS
postmarketos.desc: Real Linux on your phone. Community-driven, not Android.

replicant.name: Replicant
replicant.desc: 100% free software Android. No proprietary drivers.

ubports.name: Ubuntu Touch
ubports.desc: Ubuntu Linux for phones. Gesture-based, privacy-friendly.

romfinder.searching: Searching for compatible operating systems for your device...
romfinder.found: We found these operating systems available for your device:

norom.title: No operating system available for automatic installation
norom.explain: The main projects (LineageOS, /e/OS) do not currently offer a version for the {device}. This usually means the device is too old or too rare to have official support.
norom.reassure: But community-made versions may still exist! Check the links below to search for one.

notfound._self: We couldn't find a compatible operating system automatically.
notfound.hint: You can still choose a ROM file manually below, or search on the XDA Forums for your device model.

search.try: You can search for a compatible ROM on these sites:
search.also: You can also search for more options:

twrp.desc: Custom recovery needed to install ROMs
twrp.available: TWRP Recovery is available for your device.

dl.done: Download complete! You can now proceed with the installation.
dl.manual: You can download the file manually from

visit.hint: Download the ZIP file, then use the file browser above to select it.

romfinder.error: Could not search for compatible ROMs.

next.steps: Ready to install. Use the buttons below to proceed:

recovery._self: Step 1: Flash the recovery image (device must be in Download Mode).
recovery.hint: For Samsung: hold Power + Home + Volume Down, then Volume Up to confirm.

sideload._self: Boot into recovery, then start ADB sideload to install the ROM.
sideload.after: Step 2: Boot into the new recovery, then start ADB sideload to install the ROM.
sideload.hint: In recovery: Advanced > ADB Sideload > Swipe to start.

mode.hint: Make sure the device is in Download Mode and connected via USB.
Make sure the device is in ADB sideload mode (Recovery > Advanced > ADB Sideload).

install.complete: Installation complete! You can now reboot from recovery.

matching.preset: No matching preset found in devices.cfg

ipfs.unavailable: IPFS daemon not running. Install Kubo and run 'ipfs daemon' to enable IPFS features.
ipfs.empty: No ROMs pinned to IPFS yet. Download a ROM and it will be automatically pinned.

diag.nodevice: No device connected. Connect via USB with debugging enabled.

changelog.none: No version history found for this device.

downloadflash.title: Download and flash?
downloadflash.body: This will download the ROM and flash it to your device in one step.
